title: Containers not working unless network is slirp4netns
body:
### Issue Description

Since yesterday podman has been refusing to create new rootless containers whenever I need some kind of networking. It only works with slirp4netns and all other attempts just give me a pasta related error.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

You can just run the following commands with no previous configuration steps

```
❯ podman run --rm hello-world:latest
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't open network namespace /run/user/1000/netns/netns-8fe14d40-c1f9-1941-3f98-1a38b48490ba: Permission denied

❯ podman run --network pasta --rm hello-world:latest
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't open network namespace /run/user/1000/netns/netns-2d9d09a3-7e90-ce9b-7881-74e0e71b9487: Permission denied

❯ podman run --network bridge --rm hello-world:latest
Error: setting up Pasta: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't open network namespace /run/user/1000/containers/networks/rootless-netns/rootless-netns: Permission denied

❯ podman run --rm -p 8080:8080 hello-world:latest
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Failed to bind port 8080 (Permission denied) for option '-t 8080-8080:8080-8080', exiting

❯ podman run --rm --network slirp4netns hello-world:latest

Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.
...
```

### Describe the results you received

Containers don't start unless they have slirp4netns or no networking at all

### Describe the results you expected

Containers should start as expected

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details


I'm using Fedora Silverblue with version "40.20240606.0".
It includes podman 5.1.1 and kernel 6.8.11.


I'm also including this, in case it's useful:

```
❯ rpm -qi passt
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
My bad, I installed podman-compose using brew, which apparently pulled down it's own version of podman which was being used instead of Fedora's included one.
I assumed installing podman-compose using homebrew was fine because it's written in the docs, but maybe it's not.
